content_of_post_to_comment:
Six courses into this meditation thing and the detail that stuck with me most wasn't the sitting, it was the food. On these 10-day retreats there's no dinner. New students get fruit and tea at 5pm and that's it until breakfast. the first two evenings i was genuinely hungry, the kind where you're doing math on how many hours until oatmeal.

the standard discipline line is push through the discomfort, and sure, that's true as far as it goes. but what actually happened around day three caught me off guard. The hunger didn't go away. the alarm attached to it did. Same empty stomach, minus the little emergency siren that usually turns "i'm hungry" into "i need to eat right now."

that reframed the whole thing for me. I'd always treated discipline as out-willing the urge, white-knuckling through it. turns out most of the fight was me treating a passing feeling like an emergency that had to be solved that second. Drop the emergency framing and there's a lot less left to out-will. not a teacher here, six courses deep and still clumsy at it, but the urges i handle best now are the ones i stopped arguing with.

what's a discomfort you've quietly stopped treating as an emergency? written with ai
